Field.Label: This device canot be edited . Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
pick Gamut for this lamp:
See developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/supported-devices for supported values.
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning On this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support On transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable ON Ramp Rate

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning Off this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support Off transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable OFF Ramp Rate
Some non-Hue devices don't support ON or OFF transition times. If you find a light doesn't trun ON or OFF when it should, try checking one or both of the above check boxes.
log events and changes to indigolog

Field.tooltip: to keep a record of the serial number in case it needs to be re-learned
Field.Label: Serial Number on Lamp:
This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning On this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support On transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable ON Ramp Rate

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning Off this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support Off transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable OFF Ramp Rate
Some non-Hue devices don't support ON or OFF transition times. If you find a light doesn't trun ON or OFF when it should, try checking one or both of the above check boxes.

pick Gamut for this lamp:
See developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/supported-devices for supported values.
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Device.Name: Color Lights (Aura, Bloom, StoryLight, LivingColors, etc.)

Field.Label: This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
pick Gamut for this lamp:
See developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/supported-devices for supported values.
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning On this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support On transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable ON Ramp Rate

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning Off this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support Off transtion times.

Some non-Hue devices don't support ON or OFF transition times. If you find a light doesn't trun ON or OFF when it should, try checking one or both of the above check boxes.

Field.Label: This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning On this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support On transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable ON Ramp Rate

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning Off this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support Off transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable OFF Ramp Rate
Some non-Hue devices don't support ON or OFF transition times. If you find a light doesn't trun ON or OFF when it should, try checking one or both of the above check boxes.

This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
pick Gamut for this lamp:
See developers.meethue.com/develop/hue-api/supported-devices for supported values.
Max Lumen:
Maximum lumen output of this light at 100% brightness (default 600). The lumen device state is calculated as: brightness% x Max Lumen / 100.
Default Brightness(%):

(1 - 100 percent) Optionally set a default brightness level used whenever an ON command is sent to this light. If no value is entered, when an ON command is sent, the light will return to the brightness it was at before being turned off.
Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning On this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support On transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable ON Ramp Rate

Field.tooltip: Check this box to ignore ramp rates (transition times) when turning Off this device. Some non-Hue devices don't support Off transtion times.
Field.Label: Disable OFF Ramp Rate
Some non-Hue devices don't support ON or OFF transition times. If you find a light doesn't trun ON or OFF when it should, try checking one or both of the above check boxes.
refresh group color (Pro bridge only)

V2 hub bridge does not send updated color values for groups when a light is changed. With this option you can select (a) do not update this groups device Color (b) read bridge v1 data and fill color values (c) do a calculation with the lights of the group stored in indigo
log events and changes to indigolog
This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)

Field.tooltip: Select a temperature scale to display in Indigo.
Field.Label: Temperature Scale:

Field.tooltip: Optionally set a calibration offset if the sensor readings are inaccurate.
Field.Label: Calibration Offset:
(-10 to 10 degrees) Optionally set a calibration offset if the sensor readings are inaccurate.

log events and changes to indigolog

Field.tooltip: to keep a record of the serial number in case it needs to be re-learned
Field.Label: Serial Number on Lamp:

Field.tooltip: Select the Gateway / Bridge this device is associated with
Field.Label: Hue Bridge number:
click to
Field.Title: select GW Number

Field.tooltip: Select a Hue light or group that this attribute controller should control.
Field.Label: Hue Device:

Field.tooltip: Select an attribute associated with the Hue Device above that this attribute controller should control. Note that if you select an attribute that the Hue Device doesn't have, this attribute controller will not work.
Field.Label: Attribute to Control:
Select an attribute on the Hue device to be conrolled.

Field.tooltip: Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time) by entering the number of seconds the Hue Device selected above should take to change settings, if not overriden by the action telling it to change. Accurate to 0.1 seconds. A value of 0 makes changes instant.
Field.Label: Ramp Rate(secs):
(0 - 540 seconds) 0 = instant. Optionally override the default Ramp Rate (transition time), accurate to 0.1 seconds.

Field.tooltip: Optionally set a default On level. When turned on, the Hue Device Attribute Controller will go to the percentage specified here. If no value is specified, the control will always go to 100% when turned on by Indigo. Range is 1 to 100 percent.
Field.Label: Default On Level:

(1 to 100 percent) Optionally set a default "on" level. When turned on, the Hue Device Attribute Controller will go to the percentage specified here. If no value is specified, the control will always go to 100% when turned "on" by Indigo.
This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
log events and changes to indigolog
This is only to EDIT devices. Newly devices added to your Hue bridge/gateway are added automatically in the indigo device folder "Hue New Devices" (set name in config)
log events and changes to indigolog
